# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

```yaml
name: Node CI
on: [push]
jobs: 
  test-and-deploy:   # job name, ie "test" and "build", which are the steps below this line define in your workflow file   
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest            # specify that we want to run on latest version of Ubuntu (or any other) machine. You can change it here as needed  -  
      services:                         # add a service called "postgresql" which will be used by this job, and provide the parameters for starting up our database in one line   
       postgresql:                      # name that you want to give your PostgreSQL instance (you do not need quotes)    
        image: 'node-postgres'          # run a specific version of Ubuntu here   -  see https://hub.docker.com/_/postgres for more info on this   
       with:                            # specify the parameters and values to use in your job below as environment variables (values are passed from our step above)    
        POSTGRES_DB: myapp              # name of database - you can change it here   --  see https://hub.docker.com/_/postgres for more info on this   
       ...                            # continue to add parameters and values as needed (like port, user etc.)      ---     in the next step we will use these variables inside our job steps below: postgresql_url="${services-postgresql}->database.hostname}:5432/myapp",
       ...                            # continue to add parameters and values as needed (like port, user etc.)      ---     in the next step we will use these variables inside our job steps below: postgres_url="${services-postgresql}->database.hostname}:5432/myapp",
       ...                            # continue to add parameters and values as needed (like port, user etc.)      ---     in the next step we will use these variables inside our job steps below: postgres_user="${services-postgresql}->database.username", …    -   run is your command line tool which runs commands on Linux using bash
        POSTGRES_USERNAME=${{ secrets.POSTGRES_DB }} # name of the user to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${{ secrets[POSTGRES_DB] }} # name of the password to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_HOST="${services-postgresql}->database.hostname", # name of the host to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_PORT="${services-postgresql}->database.port", # name of the port to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_ADDON: "${services-postgresql}->database" # name of the database to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_DATABASE=myapp       # set your PostgreSQL db name - You should not need to change it here   ------ get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${{ secrets[POSTGRES_DB] }} # name of the password to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_USERNAME=${{ secrets[POSTGRES_DB] }} # name of the user to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRASP="${services-postgresql}->database", # name of the database to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${{ secrets[POSTGRES_DB] }} # name of the password to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRASP="${services-postgresql}->database", # name of the database to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_USERNAME=${{ secrets[POSTGRASP] }} # name of the user to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${{ secrets[POSTGRASP] }} # name of the password to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_HOST="${services-postgresql}->database", # name of the host to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_PORT="${services-postgresql}->database", # name of the port to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_ADDON: "${services-postgresql}->database" # name of the database to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_DATABASE=myapp       # set your PostgreSQL db name - You should not need to change it here   ------ get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${{ secrets[POSTGRASP] }} # name of the password to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_USERNAME=${{ secrets[POSTGRASP] }} # name of the user to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_HOST="${services-postgresql}->database", # name of the host to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_PORT="${services-postgresql}->database", # name of the port to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_ADDON: "${services-postgresql}->database" # name of the database to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_DATABASE=myapp       # set your PostgreSQL db name - You should not need to change it here   ------ get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${{ secrets[POSTGRASP] }} # name of the password to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_USERNAME=${{ secrets[POSTGRASP] }} # name of the user to create in database (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
        POSTGRES_HOST="${services-postgresql}->database", # name of the host to connect (you can change it here)     -- get this value from project settings when you add a secret  ---    see https://docs.github.com/en/actions for more info on GitHub Actions
